mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction demand, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deltas in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.10 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts equity valuations over a 5-day horizon, but only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent, or returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of rising INDPRO cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20-day forward returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.3 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive forward returns in greater than 65 percent of high-inflation threshold crossings, invalidating the discount rate compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, reducing recession probability and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp single-month decline in the unemployment rate signals rapid labor market tightening and imminent wage pressure, which triggers short-term equity buying momentum over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by > 0.2% in a single month
failure_condition: Short-term forward returns are negative or flat in greater than 55 percent of rapid unemployment decline events, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in walk-forward tests.
